source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
H2 Analytics is redefining how professionals train for high-stakes environments through advanced simulation and data-driven tools. We build EASE (Exercise Architect Suite), a tool used by military, intelligence, law enforcement, and emergency response organizations to develop mission-critical training exercises. Join a fast-moving team where your code powers elite performance, and innovation is part of the job.




ABOUT THE TEAM:

The core EASE team builds tools for creating and running large-scale training exercises. Our current focus is on creating generative AI features using LLMs to generate relevant training content, integration with military simulators, and an easier interface. Our stack is primarily Typescript, Vue, NestJS / Node, Mongo, Python.

The engineering team is 100% remote; our head office is located in Ottawa and you’re welcome to come in. This role can be located anywhere in Canada with flexible hours, though our core meetings are oriented on the Eastern time zone. We believe in shipping code frequently. We value collaboration. We are driven by results, not process. We use a lightweight Scrum (agile) development practice and we are dedicated to continuous improvement.




ABOUT YOU:

You are a well-rounded senior fullstack engineer with at least 6+ years of experience building complex web applications and backend services as a full-stack engineer, with a minimum of 2 years at the senior level. You love teaching, and you’re comfortable taking the lead on complex features, mentoring less-experienced developers. You’re a lifelong avid learner.




WE LOOK FOR:

 - Strong architecture and design skills

 - Advanced knowledge of reactive UI frameworks (React, Vue…)

 - Expertise with TypeScript, JavaScript, and Python

 - Experience developing REST APIs

 - Database architecture experience (SQL / NoSQL)

 - Deep experience with Linux and Docker containers

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent experience

 - This role is only open to people able to obtain Secret security clearance in Canada due to the nature of our work.


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Go, Rust

 - Experience building and using generative AI and LLMs

 - Experience architecting and maintaining production cloud infrastructure (AWS, Terraform, devops are a plus)

 - Architecting microservices and large-scale distributed systems for performance, scalability, high availability

 - Experience interviewing software engineering candidates at a senior level

 - Software security background; authentication and authorization, OWASP top 10


WHAT YOU’LL OWN:

 - Lead the architectural design and implementation of EASE features including developing APIs, UX, and automated tests

 - Gathering stakeholder feedback and defining features to meet customer needs

 - Writing proposals, carrying out code experiments, and building prototypes


WHAT YOU’LL HELP WITH:

 - Identify and push for technical and process improvements

 - Influencing what we build and how we build it

 - Improving our CI/CD and simplifying deployment and administration

 - Maintain and diagnose our software as it runs in production in the cloud or in customer data centers, in support of our customers

 - Drive performance and scalability improvements


WHAT YOU’LL TEACH:

 - Advanced coding practices and architectural patterns

 - Mentoring junior developers and fostering best practices

 - Review and approve code contributions from team members, ensuring quality, security, compliance


WHAT YOU’LL LEARN:

 - An appreciation for how defence, intelligence, and emergency response teams train for real-world scenarios

 - How to build a team in a small, fast-moving startup


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - The annual base salary for this role is between CAD $120k - $170k, based on skill and experience.

 - Extended health insurance for employees and their dependants

 - A generous and flexible vacation policy. We encourage time off to recharge

 - Co

source_jobs.title: Lead Specialist, Intelligence
source_jobs.description:
H2 Analytics is a mission-focused technology company redefining how intelligence specialists train and modernize. We partner with military, national security, law enforcement, and other mission-driven organizations to improve readiness at speed and scale. Our team includes former intelligence professionals who have lived the problems we’re solving. We move fast, have ambitious goals, and are driven by a strong sense of purpose.




THE PAIN POINT YOU CAN HELP US SOLVE

Mission-driven organizations struggle to deliver realistic training and modernize analytic workflows. Building tailored and credible scenarios with notional intelligence content is still a heavily manual and time-consuming effort that often requires pulling experienced personnel away from real operations. The result is a wide and persistent gap between the speed, scale, and realism teams need and what they actually have.

Our flagship platform, EASE, makes it simple to create synthetic intelligence environments for training and experimentation. But software alone isn’t the finish line: many clients need a partner to help shape realistic scenarios, integrate EASE into existing processes, and drive adoption in a way that realizes its full value.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re building a team of specialists who combine deep experience with the ability to turn that insight into practical solutions for our partners.

As Lead Specialist, you will sit at the intersection of delivery and product for intelligence training and modernization. You’ll own complex client outcomes with a high degree of autonomy, serve as the primary point of contact for client leads on your programs, shape how we design and deliver exercises or solutions, and translate field experience into reusable improvements. Reporting to the Vice President, you will serve as a trusted client partner, design and build synthetic intelligence environments, support adoption and change enablement, and bring operational insight back to our product team.

You’ll thrive here if you’re deeply motivated by a sense of mission. We work with teams doing high-stakes jobs, and we take that responsibility seriously. We look for good judgment, initiative, and creativity. We like solving hard problems with simple, elegant approaches, and we value people who bring new angles without ego. If you’re excited by high standards, real responsibility, and building alongside users, you will feel right at home.



Prerequisites:

10+ years of relevant experience in defence, national security, or public safety environments, to include:

 - Experience owning complex investigations and/or intelligence cycles end-to-end, operating with high autonomy in ambiguous environments.

 - Experience acting as the primary point of contact for client leads, shaping scope, managing difficult conversations, and steering engagements through ambiguity.

 - Advanced analytic or operational craft, including the ability to design approaches to non-routine problems rather than simply execute established processes.

 - Proven ability to synthesize complex inputs (e.g., technical or tactical data) into decision-ready outputs for senior stakeholders.

 - Experience reviewing others’ work, mentoring peers, and maintaining a high standard of craft across deliverables.

 - Eligible to obtain (or hold) a Government of Canada Level III (Top Secret) security clearance.

 - Willingness to travel.



Core skills:

 - Ability to act as a trusted point of contact for client leads, shape expectations, and navigate difficult conversations in high-trust environments.

 - Ability to build buy-in for new tools and workflows, address resistance constructively, and guide adoption to sustained impact.

 - Ability to take unclear problems from concept to implementation with limited oversight, making sound trade-offs between client needs and product constraints.

 - Ability to manage scope, effort, and risk in real client engagements, proactively identifying issues early and

source_jobs.title: Simulation Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
H2 Analytics is a mission-focused technology company redefining how intelligence specialists train and modernize. We partner with military, national security, law enforcement, and other mission-driven organizations to improve readiness at speed and scale. Our team includes former intelligence professionals who have lived the problems we’re solving. We move fast, have ambitious goals, and are driven by a strong sense of purpose.




THE PAIN POINT YOU CAN HELP US SOLVE

Mission-driven organizations struggle to deliver realistic training and modernize analytic workflows. Building tailored and credible scenarios with notional intelligence content is still a heavily manual and time-consuming effort that often requires pulling experienced personnel away from real operations. The result is a wide and persistent gap between the speed, scale, and realism teams need and what they actually have.

Our flagship platform, EASE, makes it simple to create synthetic intelligence environments for training and experimentation. EASE integrates with LVC simulation systems (e.g., JCATS, VBS, CPE) to model real-world environments, platforms, and behaviours. These systems are powerful, but complex; they require the right combination of technical aptitude and operational understanding (equipment, organizations, doctrine) to configure correctly and produce credible outputs.

Your work will help bridge the gap between operational military concepts and technical execution, ensuring that simulation scenarios produced for EASE accurately reflect real-world equipment, organizations, and tactical doctrine.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

As a Simulation Support Specialist, you will help build and run military simulation scenarios that support EASE-based training. Working as part of a project team, you'll contribute to the creation of high-fidelity synthetic environments by configuring entities, equipment, and behaviours within simulation platforms.

You'll support internal delivery teams and project managers, helping translate training objectives into working simulation setups and ensuring outputs are usable within EASE. Over time, you may engage directly with clients to better understand their requirements and refine scenarios, but your core focus will be on building and executing simulations that accurately reflect real-world operations.

You'll thrive here if you enjoy hands-on problem solving, have an interest in how military systems and operations are represented in simulation, and are motivated by contributing to meaningful, mission-driven work.



Prerequisites:

Relevant experience, preferably in a defence/military environment, to include:

 - Foundational understanding of global military equipment, organizational hierarchies (ORBATs), and operational doctrine.

 - Experience interpreting client training needs and translating them into technical simulation parameters.

 - Experience manipulating data structures to represent entities and equipment accurately within a software environment.

 - Experience working effectively within a project team, supporting project managers and contributing to shared outcomes.

 - Eligible to obtain (or hold) a Government of Canada Level III (Top Secret) security clearance.

 - Able to support clients on-site as required.



Core skills:

 - Ability to construct and run simulation scenarios aligned with defined training objectives and operational context, including modeling military equipment and individual entities.

 - Ability to guide clients through EASE/Simulation implementation, ensuring they understand platform capabilities and entity behaviours.

 - Ability to assist clients in refining training architectures and simulation-based methodologies to improve outcomes.

 - Ability to capture field lessons and performance data to inform product development and workflow improvements.

 - Strong written and verbal skills, with the ability to translate between operators and technical teams.



Nice to have:

 - Understandi

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
H2 Analytics is a mission-focused technology company redefining how intelligence specialists train and modernize. We partner with military, national security, law enforcement, and other mission-driven organizations to improve readiness at speed and scale. Our team includes former intelligence professionals who have lived the problems we’re solving. We move fast, have ambitious goals, and are driven by a strong sense of purpose.




THE PAIN POINT YOU CAN HELP US SOLVE

Mission-driven organizations operate in complex, highly controlled technical environments where deploying and maintaining modern software is far from straightforward. Systems are often air-gapped, security requirements are stringent, and infrastructure varies widely across organizations.

Our platform, EASE, enables large-scale, high-quality training and experimentation. Each deployment must be carefully configured, integrated with existing systems, and adapted to the constraints of secure client networks. This creates a critical gap: organizations need robust, reliable deployments, but integrating software into their environments can be time-consuming and technically demanding, with each deployment shaped by different infrastructure, security requirements, and levels of technical maturity.

We’re solving that problem by embedding our technical expertise directly into client delivery work, ensuring our software works seamlessly in their environments and enabling teams to focus on training, not infrastructure.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you will deploy, configure, and support EASE and other software solutions in client environments, primarily on-premises. Working as part of a project team, you’ll be responsible for ensuring our software integrates effectively with client infrastructure and performs reliably under operational constraints.

You’ll spend much of your time installing and configuring systems, troubleshooting issues across application, network, and infrastructure layers, and adapting deployments to meet the realities of each environment. This includes working with secure or restricted networks, integrating with third-party systems, and resolving technical challenges that don’t always have clean or predictable solutions.

You’ll work closely with project managers and engineering teams to deliver successful deployments, and engage directly with clients as needed to diagnose issues, understand constraints, and ensure long-term success. Over time, you’ll help refine how we deploy and support EASE, contributing to more scalable and repeatable delivery practices.

You’ll thrive in this role if you enjoy hands-on technical work, are comfortable operating in complex and imperfect environments, and take pride in solving difficult problems that sit at the intersection of software, infrastructure, and real-world use.



Prerequisites:

5+ years of experience deploying and integrating complex applications into client environments, including:

 - Demonstrated experience communicating with technical and non-technical stakeholders, explaining issues clearly, and building trust while working through complex problems.

 - Exceptional problem solving and debugging skills, including ability to troubleshoot complex technical issues across application, infrastructure, and network layers.

 - Strong working knowledge of Linux sysadmin, (e.g., user management, security, package managers, storage, etc.).

 - Strong working knowledge of networking configuration and best practices (firewalls, routing, VPNs) and experience troubleshooting network-related issues, ideally in secure network environments.

 - Experience with scripting (e.g., Bash, Python) to automate tasks and streamline deployments.

 - Experience with Docker / containerization and image creation.

 - Experience working directly with clients to diagnose issues, understand constraints, and support successful deployments.

 - Knowledge of hypervisor software (e.g. VMWare, Hyper-V, VSp

source_jobs.title: Intelligence Trainer (Contractor, on-call)
source_jobs.description:
H2 Analytics is a mission-focused technology company redefining how intelligence specialists train and modernize. We partner with military, national security, law enforcement, and other mission-driven organizations to improve readiness at speed and scale. Our team includes former intelligence professionals who have lived the problems we’re solving. We move fast, have ambitious goals, and are driven by a strong sense of purpose.




THE WORK

H2 Analytics supports military, law enforcement, and security partners through practical, scenario-driven intelligence training delivered in operationally relevant environments worldwide.

We are expanding our roster of contracted trainers to support short-duration international engagements on an as-needed basis. These are flexible, contract-based opportunities intended for professionals interested in supporting periodic training engagements rather than full-time employment.

Current areas of instruction include:

 - Intelligence Analysis & Tradecraft

 - Open-Source Intelligence (OSINT)

 - Online Mis/Dis/Malinformation (MDM)

 - Source Handling & Evaluation

Engagements are typically 1-2 weeks in duration and may support partners across Africa, Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and South America.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking experienced professionals with operational and instructional backgrounds in military, intelligence, law enforcement, or security environments.

Ideal candidates possess a combination of the following experience:

 - 5+ years of analytical experience within military, intelligence, law enforcement, or security environments

 - Experience with geospatial and operational platforms such as ArcGIS, TAK, or equivalent systems

 - Experience with analytical tools such as Gephi, i2 Analyst’s Notebook, Palantir, or similar platforms

 - Ability to deliver practical, scenario-driven instruction in operationally relevant environments

 - Native or professional fluency in a second language




ENGAGEMENT MODEL

This is a roster-based contractor opportunity intended for professionals interested in supporting training engagements on an as-needed basis. Engagement frequency will vary based on client requirements, regional needs, and subject-matter alignment. No minimum volume of work is guaranteed.

Successful candidates may be considered for future opportunities across training delivery, scenario development, operational support, or full-time roles as H2 Analytics continues to grow.

Depending on the nature of the engagement, contractors may be required to meet additional client or regional requirements, which can include travel readiness, insurance coverage, security screening, or other administrative prerequisites.




HOW WE USE AI

We may use AI-enabled tools to screen applications during the initial stage (for example, to identify relevant skills and experience in your resume). However, AI is not used to make final hiring decisions; all shortlisting and hiring decisions are made by our hiring team.

notes: Mission-focused tech company with a strong engineering core, partnering with military and security organizations.
